title: Rumohra cavaleriei
intro: Rumohra cavaleriei, commonly known as the Chinese Lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rumohra cavaleriei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or made into a tea, and is also used in the form of a capsule or tablet to harness its health benefits.
taxonomy: Rumohra cavaleriei is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to China and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant is a type of evergreen fern, commonly found in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Rumohra cavaleriei is within the genus Rumohra, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions.
